title: Infusion Chair And Staff Pairing
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: infusion chair and staff pairing.
assumptions: All scheduled visits are known to the scheduling system before activation.
Resource availability flags are authoritative and updated atomically by reservation/release actions.
Staff qualifications and area eligibilities are encoded in the pairwise eligibility relations.
Medication batches and equipment are tracked and cannot be double-allocated once reserved.
risks: Concurrent reservations may create contention if external systems allow stale availability states.
Staff shortages or unexpected unavailability may block essential preparatory actions and delay start.
Equipment failure (pump or monitoring station) can invalidate a reservation and require re-planning.
Patient no-show or late arrival can lead to wasted reserved medication or chair time unless released promptly.
